asi.hypothesis: Strict mode detection should scan the full directive prologue, not only the first statement.
asi.learned: Strict directive detection now skips over leading string-literal expression directives until it finds `use strict` or a non-directive statement.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible strict-mode diagnostics for a `use strict` directive following another directive string.

asi.hypothesis: A `use strict` string after a non-directive statement should not make the function strict.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after directive-prologue scanning; scanning stops at the first non-string directive statement, so duplicate parameters remain valid in this non-strict function.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible function coverage for non-prologue `"use strict"` string after a call expression.

asi.hypothesis: Strict restricted-name diagnostics should apply to object accessor parameters.
asi.learned: Object accessor parsing now runs strict parameter validation for getter/setter function bodies.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible strict-mode diagnostics for `set value(eval) { "use strict"; }` in object literals.

asi.hypothesis: Strict restricted-name diagnostics should apply to class accessor parameters after accessor validation support.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after class accessor validation; setter parameters named `arguments` are rejected in strict accessor bodies.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible strict-mode diagnostics for `class C { set value(arguments) { "use strict"; } }`.

description: Port strict default parameter duplicate diagnostics

asi.hypothesis: Strict duplicate-parameter diagnostics should include assignment-pattern parameters with default values.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after recursive binding-name collection; AssignmentPattern left identifiers participate in duplicate detection.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible strict-mode diagnostics for duplicate default parameters `a = 1, a = 2`.

asi.hypothesis: Strict restricted-name diagnostics should include rest parameter identifiers.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after recursive binding-name collection; RestElement arguments participate in restricted-name validation.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible strict-mode diagnostics for `function f(...arguments) { "use strict"; }`.

description: Port class method implicit strict diagnostics

asi.hypothesis: Class method bodies are strict code, so duplicate parameters should be rejected even without an explicit `use strict` directive.
asi.learned: Class method parsing now validates parameter lists as strict unconditionally for regular, generator, async, and accessor methods.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class diagnostics for `class C { method(a, a) { return a; } }`.

description: Port class generator implicit strict diagnostics

asi.hypothesis: Generator class methods should also reject duplicate parameters because class method bodies are strict code.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after unconditional class method parameter validation; generator class methods share the same strict validation path.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class diagnostics for `class C { *method(a, a) { yield a; } }`.

description: Port class accessor implicit strict diagnostics

asi.hypothesis: Class accessor parameters should reject restricted names because class method bodies are strict even without directive prologues.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after unconditional class accessor validation; setter parameters named `eval` are rejected.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class diagnostics for `class C { set value(eval) { this.value = eval; } }`.

asi.hypothesis: Regexp lexical-goal heuristics should allow regexp literals after control-flow delimiters such as if/while conditions and switch case labels.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; regexp literals in these control-flow positions are tokenized and parsed through normal expression parsing.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible regexp coverage in `if`, `while`, and `switch case` expression contexts.

asi.hypothesis: Regexp lexical-goal heuristics should allow regexp literals after conditional and logical operators.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; regexp literals after `?`, `:`, `||`, and `&&` parse as expression operands.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible regexp coverage for conditional branches and logical-expression operands.

asi.hypothesis: Regexp lexical-goal heuristics should allow regexp literals as right-hand operands of assignment and logical-assignment operators.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; assignment operator contexts parse regexp literals as right-hand AssignmentExpression operands.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible regexp coverage after `=`, `||=`, and `??=` operators.

asi.hypothesis: Regexp scanner should report unterminated regexp literals at EOF or before a line terminator.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; lexer diagnostics already report `unterminated regular expression literal` for EOF and newline termination.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible regexp diagnostics for EOF-terminated and newline-terminated regexp literals.

asi.hypothesis: Template literal scanning should report unterminated templates at EOF.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; template scanning emits `unterminated template literal` while preserving the partial template literal token for recovery.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible template literal diagnostic coverage for EOF before closing backtick.

asi.hypothesis: String literal scanning should report unterminated strings at EOF or before an unescaped line terminator.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; string scanning emits `unterminated string literal` for EOF and newline termination while recovering with the cooked prefix.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible string literal diagnostics for EOF-terminated and newline-terminated strings.

asi.hypothesis: Static import declarations should require a string literal module source after `from`.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; module source parsing emits `expected module source` when `from` is followed by an identifier.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module diagnostics for default, named, and namespace imports with non-string sources.

asi.hypothesis: Re-export declarations should require a string literal module source after `from`.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; named and export-all source parsing share module source diagnostics for non-string sources.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module diagnostics for named, export-all, and namespace re-exports with non-string sources.

asi.hypothesis: Static import declarations with specifiers should require a `from` keyword before the module source.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; import parsing emits `expected from` when default, named, or namespace specifiers are followed directly by a string source.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module diagnostics for default, named, and namespace imports missing `from`.

asi.hypothesis: Arrow functions should reject duplicate parameter names even with expression bodies and without a `use strict` directive.
asi.learned: Arrow parameter validation now always applies duplicate-name checks, while still using directive-sensitive validation for restricted names in strict block bodies.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for duplicate parameters in sync and async arrow functions.

asi.hypothesis: Arrow duplicate-parameter diagnostics should inspect nested binding patterns, not only simple parameters.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after recursive binding-name collection and unconditional arrow duplicate checks; destructured arrow parameters now participate in duplicate detection.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for duplicate names across object and array destructuring arrow parameters.

description: Port async line terminator arrow coverage

asi.hypothesis: A line terminator after `async` should prevent it from being parsed as an async arrow-function modifier.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; async arrow lookahead already checks token line-terminator metadata and falls back to an identifier expression followed by a normal arrow expression statement.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible async-arrow coverage for `async\nx => x`.

asi.hypothesis: Function and arrow formal parameter parsers should accept trailing commas before the closing parenthesis.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; parameter-list parsing already stops cleanly on `)` after a comma for function declarations and parenthesized arrows.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible function coverage for `function f(a, b,)` and `(a, b,) => a`.

asi.hypothesis: Rest parameters should reject trailing commas in function and arrow parameter lists.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; the parameter parser expects `)` immediately after a rest binding and reports parser errors for comma forms.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `function f(...rest,) {}` and `(...rest,) => rest`.

asi.hypothesis: Classic for-loop parsing should support omitted init, test, and update clauses.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; classic for-tail parsing preserves nil init/test/update fields for `for (;;)`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible control-flow coverage for `for (;;) { break; }`.

description: Port for loop sequence clause coverage

asi.hypothesis: Classic for-loop init and update clauses should preserve comma sequence expressions.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; expression parsing in for-loop clauses already produces SequenceExpression nodes for comma-separated assignment/update operands.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible control-flow coverage for sequence init/update clauses in a classic for loop.

asi.hypothesis: For-await-of parsing should compose with const object destructuring bindings inside async functions.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; for-await parsing reuses the for-of declaration left-hand parser and preserves `await: true`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible async control-flow coverage for `for await (const { value } of stream)`.

asi.hypothesis: Nested labeled statements should compose with for/while loops and labeled break/continue targets.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; labeled statements preserve identifier labels and break/continue statements preserve optional label identifiers.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible control-flow coverage for nested labels with `continue outer` and `break inner`.

asi.hypothesis: Empty statements should parse both as standalone statements and as loop/branch bodies.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure failed in known unrelated Process.monitor flake (`noproc` instead of `kaboom`) while parser tests passed.
asi.next_action_hint: Re-add empty statement coverage and rerun; keep if checks pass.
asi.quickjs_cases: Attempted QuickJS-compatible coverage for standalone empty statements and empty loop/if bodies.

asi.hypothesis: Empty statements should parse both as standalone statements and as loop/branch bodies.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; empty statements are represented as EmptyStatement and can appear as statement bodies for while/if branches.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible coverage for standalone empty statements and empty loop/if bodies after retrying a transient web API flake.

asi.hypothesis: Throw statements should report a diagnostic when a line terminator appears before the thrown expression.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; throw parsing emits `line terminator after throw`, returns a nil argument, and continues parsing the following expression statement.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostic coverage for `throw\nerror`.

asi.hypothesis: Return statements should apply ASI when a line terminator appears before an expression.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; return parsing preserves nil argument before a line terminator and parses the next line as a separate expression statement.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible ASI coverage for `return\nvalue` inside a function.

asi.hypothesis: Yield expressions should apply ASI when a line terminator appears before the yielded expression.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure failed in known unrelated Process.monitor flake while parser tests passed.
asi.next_action_hint: Re-add yield line terminator coverage and rerun; keep if checks pass.
asi.quickjs_cases: Attempted QuickJS-compatible ASI coverage for `yield\nvalue` inside a generator.

asi.hypothesis: Yield expressions should apply ASI when a line terminator appears before the yielded expression.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; yield parsing returns an expression with nil argument before a line terminator and the following identifier becomes a separate statement.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible ASI coverage for `yield\nvalue` inside a generator after retrying a transient web API flake.

asi.hypothesis: Do-while parsing should accept omitted trailing semicolons and continue with the next statement.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; do-while parsing consumes an optional semicolon and leaves the following identifier as a separate expression statement.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible control-flow coverage for `do { ... } while (test)` without a semicolon.

asi.hypothesis: Switch parsing should allow the default clause to appear between case clauses while preserving order.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; switch case parsing stores clauses in source order and represents default with nil test regardless of position.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible switch coverage for `case`, `default`, then another `case`.

asi.hypothesis: Switch parsing should report duplicate default clauses while still recovering the full switch body.
asi.learned: Switch case parsing now tracks whether a default clause was already seen and emits `duplicate default clause` on later defaults while preserving all clauses.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible switch diagnostics for two default clauses separated by a case.

asi.hypothesis: Class parsing should report duplicate non-static constructor methods while preserving both method nodes for recovery.
asi.learned: Class tail parsing now validates the parsed class body and emits `duplicate constructor` when more than one MethodDefinition has kind `:constructor`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class diagnostics for two constructor methods in one class body.

asi.hypothesis: A static method named `constructor` should not count as a duplicate class constructor.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after duplicate-constructor validation; static `constructor` methods keep kind `:method` and are excluded from duplicate constructor counting.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class coverage for a real constructor plus a static method named `constructor`.

asi.hypothesis: Class accessors named `constructor` should not count as duplicate constructors when a real constructor is present.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; duplicate-constructor validation only counts MethodDefinition nodes with kind `:constructor`, excluding `:get`/`:set` accessors.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class coverage for a constructor plus a getter named `constructor`.

description: Port break continue line terminator coverage

asi.hypothesis: Break and continue statements should apply ASI when a line terminator appears before a potential label identifier.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; break/continue parsing checks token line-terminator metadata and leaves the next-line identifier as a separate expression statement.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible ASI coverage for `break\nlabel` and `continue\nlabel`.

asi.hypothesis: Try statement parsing should support a finalizer without a catch clause.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; try parsing preserves nil handler and a populated finalizer block for `try ... finally`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible control-flow coverage for `try { work(); } finally { cleanup(); }`.

asi.hypothesis: Try statement parsing should support catch and finally clauses together in source order.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; try parsing preserves block, catch clause with parameter, and finalizer block in a single TryStatement.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible control-flow coverage for `try ... catch (error) ... finally ...`.

asi.hypothesis: Try statements without catch or finally should report a syntax diagnostic while preserving the try block for recovery.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; try parsing emits `expected catch or finally` and keeps a TryStatement with nil handler/finalizer.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `try { work(); }` without catch/finally.

asi.hypothesis: Optional catch binding coverage may add focused QuickJS-compatible parser coverage.
asi.rollback_reason: The primary metric did not increase because an optional catch binding test already existed; keeping would be metric gaming rather than meaningful coverage growth.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid replacing existing parser coverage; add only genuinely new focused QuickJS-derived cases.
asi.learned: Optional catch binding is already represented in the current parser test tree.

asi.hypothesis: A catch clause with empty parentheses should be diagnosed rather than treated as optional catch binding.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; catch binding parsing emits `expected binding identifier` for `catch ()` and recovers a catch clause node.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `try { ... } catch () { ... }`.

asi.hypothesis: Reserved words should be accepted as object literal property names and method names even when they are invalid binding identifiers.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; property-key parsing accepts keyword tokens for data properties and methods.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible object literal coverage for reserved keys `default`, `class`, and method name `import`.

asi.hypothesis: Reserved words should be accepted as class member names for methods, fields, and static methods.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; class property-key parsing accepts keyword tokens across method, field, and static member forms.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class coverage for reserved member names `default`, `class`, and static `import`.

asi.hypothesis: Object literal parsing should report duplicate non-computed data properties named `__proto__` while preserving the object AST for recovery.
asi.learned: Object expression parsing now validates completed property lists and emits `duplicate __proto__ property` when multiple non-computed data properties use the prototype special name.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `{ __proto__: first, "__proto__": second }`.

asi.hypothesis: Only non-computed data properties named `__proto__` should participate in duplicate-prototype diagnostics; methods and computed keys should remain valid.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after duplicate-proto validation; method and computed `__proto__` properties are excluded from the duplicate data-property count.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible object literal coverage for `__proto__` data property plus method and computed forms.

asi.hypothesis: A shorthand property named `__proto__` should not count as a prototype data property duplicate.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; duplicate-proto validation excludes shorthand properties and still permits one prototype data property in the same object literal.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible object literal coverage for `{ __proto__, __proto__: base }`.

asi.hypothesis: Side-effect-only import declarations should accept import attributes and attach them to the ImportDeclaration AST.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; import declaration parsing handles attributes for empty-specifier side-effect imports.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for `import "dep" with { type: "json" };`.

asi.hypothesis: Side-effect-only import declarations should also accept legacy assertion attributes.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; module attribute parsing accepts `assert` as well as `with` after side-effect import sources.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for `import "dep" assert { type: "json" };`.

asi.hypothesis: Named re-export declarations should preserve legacy assertion attributes together with aliased export specifiers.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; export source and attribute parsing handles `assert` after named re-exports.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for `export { name as alias } from "dep" assert { ... };`.

asi.hypothesis: Export-all declarations should preserve legacy assertion attributes on the declaration AST.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; export-all parsing already attaches `assert` attributes to ExportAllDeclaration.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for `export * from "dep" assert { type: "json" };`.

asi.hypothesis: Namespace export-all declarations should preserve legacy assertion attributes and exported alias names together.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; export-all parsing handles `* as namespace` and attaches assertion attributes.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for `export * as namespace from "dep" assert { ... };`.

asi.hypothesis: Class field initializers and method bodies should preserve `new.target` and chained `import.meta` meta-property expressions.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; class member expression parsing keeps meta-property AST nodes in fields and member-expression chains.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class coverage for `field = new.target` and `return import.meta.url`.

asi.hypothesis: Class methods should preserve `super.property` member expressions when parsing derived classes.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure failed in the known unrelated Process.monitor flake: expected `kaboom`, got `noproc`, while parser tests passed.
asi.next_action_hint: Re-add the super member access coverage and rerun; keep only if web API checks pass.
asi.quickjs_cases: Attempted QuickJS-compatible class coverage for `return super.value` in a derived class method.

asi.hypothesis: Class methods should preserve `super.property` member expressions when parsing derived classes.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; `super` is represented as an identifier object in the member expression and the derived superclass identifier is preserved.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class coverage for `class C extends B { method() { return super.value; } }` after retrying a transient web API flake.

asi.hypothesis: Derived class methods should preserve computed super member calls in the parser AST.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; computed member access on `super` is parsed as a MemberExpression callee inside a CallExpression.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class coverage for `return super[expr]();`.

asi.hypothesis: Derived class constructors should preserve direct `super(...)` calls and arguments in the AST.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; `super(value)` parses as a CallExpression with callee identifier `super` inside a constructor method body.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class coverage for `constructor(value) { super(value); }`.

asi.hypothesis: Object expression parsing should preserve spread elements among data and shorthand properties.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; object literal parsing represents `...rest` as a SpreadElement in property order.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible object literal coverage for `{ a: 1, ...rest, b }`.

asi.hypothesis: Object binding patterns should diagnose a rest element followed by another binding property while recovering the remaining pattern.
asi.learned: Object pattern parsing now reports `rest element must be last` when a comma follows a rest element and continues parsing later properties for recovery.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible destructuring diagnostics for `var { ...rest, after } = object;`.

asi.hypothesis: Array binding patterns should diagnose a rest element followed by another binding element while recovering the remaining pattern.
asi.learned: Array pattern parsing now reports `rest element must be last` when a comma follows a rest element and continues parsing later elements for recovery.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible destructuring diagnostics for `var [first, ...rest, after] = value;`.

asi.hypothesis: `await` should be rejected as a binding identifier when parsing module source, even though it remains allowed in script bindings.
asi.learned: Binding identifier parsing now checks parser source_type and emits the existing binding diagnostic for keyword `await` in modules.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module diagnostics for `var await;` with `source_type: :module`.

asi.hypothesis: Strict function bodies should reject declaration/expression binding names `eval` and `arguments`.
asi.learned: Function declaration/expression parsing now validates restricted function names when the body directive prologue contains `use strict`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible strict diagnostics for `function eval() { "use strict"; }` and named function expression `arguments`.

asi.hypothesis: A top-level strict directive should reject `eval` and `arguments` as program binding names.
asi.learned: Program parsing now validates top-level variable/function/class binding names when the directive prologue contains `use strict`, reusing binding-name extraction for patterns.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible strict diagnostics for top-level `var eval` and `function arguments()`.

description: Port strict function body binding diagnostics

asi.hypothesis: Strict function bodies should reject `eval` and `arguments` declarations in the function body, not only in parameter lists or function names.
asi.learned: Strict function-parameter validation now also checks top-level bindings parsed from the strict function body using the same restricted-name helper.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible strict diagnostics for `var eval` and nested `function arguments()` inside a strict function body.

asi.hypothesis: Strict function body validation should include catch binding parameters such as `catch (eval)`.
asi.learned: Strict body binding collection now walks blocks, control-flow statements, switch clauses, and try/catch/finally, including catch parameters while avoiding nested function bodies.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible strict diagnostics for `catch (eval)` inside a strict function.

asi.hypothesis: Strict body binding validation should include declarations introduced by for-in loop heads.
asi.learned: The recursive strict binding collector handles ForInStatement left declarations, so `var arguments in object` is reported under a strict function body.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible strict diagnostics for `for (var arguments in object)` inside a strict function.

asi.hypothesis: Strict body binding validation should include declarations inside switch case consequents.
asi.learned: The recursive strict binding collector handles SwitchStatement cases, so restricted names declared in case consequents are diagnosed.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible strict diagnostics for `switch (...) { case 1: var eval; }` inside a strict function.

description: Port strict class body binding diagnostics

asi.hypothesis: Class method bodies and static blocks are strict contexts and should reject body bindings named `eval` or `arguments`.
asi.learned: Class method/accessor parsing and static block parsing now validate strict body bindings, reusing the recursive body binding collector.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `var eval` inside a class method and `var arguments` inside a static block.

description: Port strict object method body binding diagnostics

asi.hypothesis: Object methods with a strict directive should reject restricted body bindings just like regular strict functions.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after strict function body binding validation; object method parsing reuses validate_strict_function_params for directive-sensitive strict body checks.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `object = { method() { "use strict"; var eval; } };`.

description: Port strict arrow body binding diagnostics

asi.hypothesis: Arrow functions with a strict directive body should reject restricted body bindings.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; arrow parsing reuses strict function validation and now benefits from recursive body binding checks.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `() => { "use strict"; var arguments; }`.

asi.hypothesis: Modules are strict contexts and should reject top-level `eval` and `arguments` binding names even without a strict directive.
asi.learned: Program strict binding validation now treats `source_type: :module` as strict in addition to explicit directive prologues.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module diagnostics for `var eval;` and `function arguments() {}` in module source.

description: Port module nested strict binding diagnostics

asi.hypothesis: Module strict binding validation should include nested statement bindings such as declarations inside block statements.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after module strict mode validation; the recursive binding collector catches `let eval` inside an if-block.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module diagnostics for `if (enabled) { let eval = value; }`.

asi.hypothesis: Strict script/function contexts should reject with-statements while preserving parsed statement AST for recovery.
asi.learned: Strict validation now traverses statement bodies and emits `with statement not allowed in strict mode` for strict program and function bodies.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for top-level strict `with` and function-body strict `with`.

asi.hypothesis: Module source is strict and should reject with-statements without requiring an explicit directive.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after module strict validation; source_type module now feeds strict with-statement checks.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module diagnostics for `with (object) { value; }`.

asi.hypothesis: Class method bodies are strict and should reject with-statements even without a strict directive.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after class strict body validation; class methods feed implicit strict with-statement checks.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `with` inside a class method body.

asi.hypothesis: Strict code should reject `delete identifier` while allowing `delete object.property`.
asi.learned: Strict validation now traverses statement expressions and reports `delete of identifier not allowed in strict mode` for delete unary expressions whose argument is an identifier.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible strict diagnostics for `delete value` and allowance coverage for `delete object.value`.

asi.hypothesis: Module source should reject `delete identifier` because modules are strict contexts.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after strict delete validation; module strict mode feeds the same delete-identifier check.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module diagnostics for `delete value;`.

asi.hypothesis: Class method bodies should reject `delete identifier` because class bodies are strict contexts.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after class strict body validation; class methods feed the strict delete-identifier traversal.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `delete value` inside a class method.

asi.hypothesis: Strict code should reject legacy decimal-looking octal integer literals while sloppy scripts remain accepted.
asi.learned: Strict validation now traverses statement expressions and reports `legacy octal literal not allowed in strict mode` for numeric literal raw forms like `010`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible strict diagnostics for `"use strict"; value = 010;` plus sloppy allowance coverage.

asi.hypothesis: Module source should reject legacy octal literals because modules are strict contexts.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after strict legacy-octal validation; module strict mode feeds the same literal raw-form check.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module diagnostics for `value = 010;`.

asi.hypothesis: Class method bodies should reject legacy octal literals because class method code is strict.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after class strict body validation; class method return expressions feed strict legacy-octal checks.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `return 010` inside a class method.

asi.hypothesis: Strict code should reject assignments and compound assignments targeting `eval` or `arguments`.
asi.learned: Strict validation now traverses statement expressions and reports `restricted assignment target in strict mode` for assignment expressions whose left side is `eval` or `arguments`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for strict `eval = value` and `arguments += value`.

asi.hypothesis: Strict code should reject prefix and postfix update expressions targeting `eval` or `arguments`.
asi.learned: Strict restricted-target validation now treats UpdateExpression targets named `eval` or `arguments` like assignment targets.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for strict `eval++` and `++arguments`.

asi.hypothesis: Module source should reject update expressions targeting `eval` because modules are strict contexts.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after strict update target validation; module strict mode feeds the same restricted-target checks.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module diagnostics for `eval++;`.

asi.hypothesis: Strict destructuring assignments should reject `eval` and `arguments` within object or array assignment targets.

asi.hypothesis: Optional chains should be rejected as assignment and update targets while preserving expression AST recovery.
asi.learned: Assignment and update parsing now detect optional-chain member/call expressions in the target and emit `optional chain is not a valid assignment target`.

asi.learned: No implementation change was required after recursive optional-chain target detection; optional CallExpression targets are diagnosed.

description: Port optional chain tagged template diagnostics

asi.hypothesis: Optional chains should be rejected as tagged template callees while regular tagged templates remain accepted.
asi.learned: Tagged template postfix parsing now emits `optional chain not allowed as tagged template callee` when the tag expression contains an optional chain.

asi.hypothesis: For-of declaration heads should report a diagnostic when the declaration includes an initializer.
asi.learned: For-in/of tail parsing now validates variable declaration heads and reports `for-in/of declaration cannot have initializer`; for-in with initializer still needs better lexical-goal recovery because `in` is currently consumed as a binary operator in the initializer expression.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `for (let value = first of values) {}`.
asi.deferred: Improve for-in declaration initializer recovery so `for (var key = first in object)` becomes a ForInStatement diagnostic instead of classic-for recovery errors.

asi.hypothesis: Rest elements in array/object binding patterns and formal parameter lists should reject initializers.
asi.learned: Rest parsing now emits `rest element cannot have initializer` when `=` follows a rest binding before the expected closing delimiter.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `var [...rest = value]`, `var { ...rest = value }`, and `function f(...rest = value)`.

asi.hypothesis: Break and continue statements should be diagnosed when they appear outside valid loop/switch contexts.
asi.learned: Program validation now walks statement bodies with loop/switch context and reports unlabelled `break` outside loop/switch plus `continue` outside loops, including switch consequents.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for top-level `break`, top-level `continue`, and `continue` inside switch without an enclosing loop.

asi.hypothesis: Labelled break/continue should be validated against visible labels, and labelled continue should require an iteration label.
asi.learned: Control-flow validation now tracks visible labels and whether each labels an iteration statement, reporting undefined break labels and non-iteration continue labels.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `break missing`, `continue label` to a block label, and allowance for `continue loop` to a loop label.

asi.hypothesis: Replacing whole-template literal tokens with TemplateLiteral/TemplateElement AST nodes closes a major compatibility gap before adding more narrow syntax diagnostics.
asi.learned: Template tokens are now converted into TemplateLiteral AST nodes with quasis and parsed embedded expressions; tagged templates now carry structured quasi nodes, including nested template expressions.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible coverage for template literal quasis/expressions and tagged template quasi AST, while updating older whole-template expectations.
asi.compat_gap: Template literals moved from raw token preservation toward structured ESTree-like shape, one of the largest parser AST fidelity gaps.

description: Convert destructuring assignment targets to patterns

asi.hypothesis: Destructuring assignment left-hand sides should use ObjectPattern/ArrayPattern and RestElement/AssignmentPattern nodes instead of expression literal nodes for AST compatibility.
asi.learned: Assignment node construction now converts object/array expression targets into pattern nodes recursively, including spread-to-rest and nested default assignment expressions to AssignmentPattern.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible AST-shape coverage for nested object/array destructuring assignment patterns and updates earlier expression-shaped expectations.
asi.compat_gap: Destructuring assignment AST fidelity moved closer to parser compatibility rather than preserving expression placeholders.

description: Require module source for import export declarations

asi.hypothesis: Static import/export declarations should be diagnosed when parsing script source, while module-source tests remain valid with source_type: :module.
asi.learned: Program validation now reports `import/export declarations only allowed in modules` for module declarations outside module source; earlier assertion/attribute module tests were corrected to parse as modules.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for script-source `import value from "dep"` and `export var value = 1`.
asi.compat_gap: Module/script source type validation now covers a broad grammar boundary instead of accepting all module syntax in scripts.

asi.hypothesis: No-substitution templates should produce a TemplateLiteral with one tail quasi and no expressions after the structured template AST change.

asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `(a + b) = value`, `call() = value`, and `call()++`.

asi.learned: Control-flow validation now emits `return statement not within function` for returns reached from program/block validation; function bodies are not traversed by that validator, so valid function returns remain accepted.

asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for module `yield value;` and `function f() { yield value; }`.

asi.hypothesis: Await expressions should be accepted at module top level and inside async functions, but diagnosed in script or non-async function contexts.

asi.hypothesis: `new.target` should parse as a meta-property but be diagnosed outside function contexts.

asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `new.target;` and `let value = new.target;`.

asi.hypothesis: `import.meta` should parse as a meta-property but be diagnosed when script source uses it outside modules.
asi.learned: Program validation now leaves module-source import.meta untouched, but scans script statement/expression trees for `import.meta` meta-properties and reports `import.meta only allowed in modules`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `let url = import.meta.url;` and `function f() { return import.meta.url; }` in script source.

asi.hypothesis: `super` calls and property access should be preserved in partial ASTs but diagnosed outside class method contexts.
asi.learned: Program validation now scans top-level and regular function statement/expression trees for `super` identifier usage while skipping class declarations, reporting `super not allowed outside class method`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `super();` and `function f() { return super.value; }`.

asi.hypothesis: Direct `super()` calls should be diagnosed unless they appear in a derived class constructor.
asi.learned: Program validation now inspects class methods for direct super calls, allowing only constructors with an `extends` clause while preserving super property access in methods.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `class C { constructor() { super(); } }` and `class C extends B { method() { super(); } }`.

description: Port class element super call diagnostics

asi.hypothesis: Direct `super()` calls should also be rejected from class static blocks and field initializers, not only ordinary methods.
asi.learned: Class super-call validation now checks static block statement bodies and field initializer expressions for direct `super()` calls.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `class C extends B { static { super(); } }` and `class C extends B { field = super(); }`.

asi.hypothesis: Class private names should be unique, except for the standard getter/setter pair shape.
asi.learned: Class validation now collects private field/method/accessor names and reports `duplicate private name` for duplicate field/method declarations while allowing complementary private accessor pairs.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `class C { #value; #value; }` and `class C { #value() {} #value() {} }`.

asi.hypothesis: Private accessors should reject duplicate getters or duplicate setters but allow a complementary getter/setter pair.
asi.learned: Existing private-name validation already handles accessor-kind uniqueness correctly: duplicate private getters are rejected, while get/set pairs parse successfully.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible coverage for `class C { get #value() {} get #value() {} }` and `class C { get #value() {} set #value(value) {} }`.

asi.hypothesis: Private names used inside a class should be declared by that class before the parser accepts the class without diagnostics.
asi.learned: Class validation now collects declared private names from fields/methods/accessors and scans field initializers, methods, and static blocks for undeclared private identifiers. Existing private accessor syntax tests now declare their backing private slot explicitly.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `this.#missing` and `#missing in this` inside class methods.

asi.hypothesis: Private identifiers outside any class body should be diagnosed as undeclared private names.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure failed in known unrelated Process.monitor flake while parser tests passed.

description: Port private name outside class diagnostics

asi.hypothesis: Private identifiers outside any class body should be diagnosed as undeclared private names.
asi.learned: Declared-private-name validation now treats non-class program statements as having an empty private-name environment, so member/private-in expressions outside classes report `undeclared private name`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `object.#missing;` and `#missing in object;` after retrying a transient web API flake.

description: Port block lexical var conflict diagnostics

asi.hypothesis: Lexical declarations should conflict with var declarations within block/function-body validation, not only at the program top level.
asi.learned: Block parsing now reuses lexical-vs-var conflict validation and the older duplicate-only helper was removed, preserving duplicate lexical diagnostics while adding block var conflicts.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `{ let value; var value; }` and `function f() { const value = 1; var value; }`.

description: Port catch parameter lexical conflict diagnostics

asi.hypothesis: Catch parameter binding names should conflict with lexical declarations in the catch body.
asi.learned: Catch clause parsing now compares binding names from the optional catch parameter with direct lexical declarations in the catch body and emits `catch parameter conflicts with lexical declaration`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for `try {} catch (error) { let error; }` and destructured catch parameter conflict with `const error`.

asi.hypothesis: Static import local names should participate in module lexical duplicate/conflict diagnostics.
asi.learned: Lexical binding collection now includes local identifiers from default, namespace, and named import specifiers, so duplicate imports and import-vs-let conflicts report duplicate lexical declarations.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for duplicate local import bindings and `import value from "a"; let value;`.

description: Optimize parser token access and lexer hot paths

asi.hypothesis: eprof-identified O(n²) token position lookup and list-based token access dominate parser time on larger inputs.
asi.profile_before: eprof on an 80-class corpus showed Enum.drop_list/2 at 52.6%, Enum.reduce list fold at 29.9%, and Lexer.position_for/2 path at ~45%+ due recomputing line/column from source start per token; parser current/peek used Enum.at/List.last repeatedly.
asi.changes: Lexer now records token start line/column when scanning begins, eliminating position_for/2; parser stores tokens as a tuple with cached count/last token for O(1) current/peek/advance; punctuator scanning uses direct binary pattern matching instead of Enum.find/String.starts_with over all punctuators; added benchmark script and shifted autoresearch segment to parser performance.
asi.benchmark_after: bench/js_parser_vs_quickjs.exs: test_language_parser_us=4386, class_private_parser_us=49, function_control_parser_us=49. Earlier manual baseline before fixes was ~304497µs on test_language.js and ~956086µs on a 25KB generated class corpus; after fixes the generated corpus was ~8213µs.
asi.quickjs_comparison: Current test_language parse is ~1.7x slower than QuickJS compile including NIF/bytecode overhead; generated valid class corpus is now faster than QuickJS compile in the manual benchmark.
asi.next_action_hint: Re-run eprof after this commit; remaining hot spots are ordinary lexer/parser dispatch such as keyword?/match_value?, current/peek, scan_identifier_parts, and validation passes.

asi.hypothesis: After eliminating O(n²) token positioning and list token access, eprof showed lexer advance helpers still contributed measurable overhead through Range/Enum construction and UTF-8 binary allocation.
asi.profile_before: Post-fix eprof top functions included Lexer.peek/current/scan_identifier_parts/advance and advance_bytes-related Range/Enum calls. Advance itself remained ~4.9% of profiled time.
asi.changes: Replaced advance_bytes/2 Enum.reduce over ranges with direct recursion and replaced byte_size(<<ch::utf8>>) allocation with utf8_size/1 guards.
asi.benchmark_after: test_language_parser_us improved from 4386 to 4313; parser suite remained 421 tests, 0 failures; backpressure checks passed.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining profile is dominated by normal scanner/parser dispatch (Lexer.peek/current, skip_trivia, Parser.current/token_at, match_value?/keyword?). Further gains likely require structural changes such as caching current token in parser state or reducing repeated current/peek calls.

asi.hypothesis: Caching current and three lookahead tokens in parser state would reduce current/peek/token_at overhead enough to improve parser throughput.
asi.result: Regression: test_language_parser_us worsened from best 4313/4386 range to 4645, class_private/function_control also worsened to 51/53, despite tests and checks passing.
asi.rollback_reason: Refreshing four cached tokens on every advance costs more than the saved elem(tuple, index) lookups; added larger parser state updates appear to dominate.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry broad lookahead caching. If revisiting, only cache current_token and maybe next_token, or instead reduce repeated current/peek calls locally in hot helpers without mutating parser state on every advance.
asi.profiling_context: Before this experiment, remaining eprof hot spots were normal dispatch: Parser.current/token_at/match_value?/keyword? and Lexer.peek/current/scan_identifier_parts.

description: Try local parser token helper simplification

asi.hypothesis: Reducing repeated current/peek helper work locally, without cached parser state, could improve hot keyword?/peek_value? dispatch.
asi.result: No primary improvement: test_language_parser_us=4409, worse than the 4313 kept best and slightly worse than the segment baseline; small snippets improved but primary regressed.
asi.rollback_reason: The pattern-matching keyword?/2 and peek_value clauses did not improve the large-file primary benchmark; likely noise or extra function clause dispatch offsets savings.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid micro-optimizing Parser.current/keyword? helpers without a profiler-backed targeted call-site change. Focus next on bigger structural work such as collapsing validation passes or optimizing lexer current/peek.
